spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the canonical `SpeechRecognition.start(track)` overload defined by the Web Speech API spec.

interactive.rationale: Enumerates input devices into a real `<select>`, fires `getUserMedia`, calls `rec.start(track)`, and renders the live transcript with interim styling.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index blurb promises a mic picker handing a chosen track to SpeechRecognition with a live transcript — the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Includes a try/catch fallback to `rec.start()` if the overload throws, but doesn't demonstrate permission denied, no-input-devices, or what happens when the track ends mid-recognition.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from CSS variables; pills use ok/bad semantic states; no inline hex or static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Show the permission-denied path
openQuestions.detail: If the user blocks mic access, `getUserMedia` rejects with `NotAllowedError`. The page currently logs `start failed: ${e.name}` but doesn't surface a user-facing recovery message or a screenshot of the chrome permission UI. Add a banner explaining how to re-grant in chrome://settings/content/microphone.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the track-ended edge case
openQuestions.detail: When the underlying MediaStreamTrack ends (device unplugged, tab close), what does the recognizer do? Wire up `track.onended` and show whether `rec.onend` fires automatically or whether the recognizer hangs.

openQuestions.title: Surface the language attribute
openQuestions.detail: The code sets `rec.lang = navigator.language || 'en-US'` silently. Expose this as a dropdown so users can see the effect of switching language on recognition quality — it's a key part of the API surface.

summary: Solid mic-picker demo that uses the canonical `SpeechRecognition.start(track)` shape. Remaining work is limited to user-facing failure and track-ending polish.
